idea.title: Token Budget
idea.hook: Route tasks through cheap or expensive AI agents on a hex grid, finishing the queue before your token budget zeroes out.
idea.prototype: Top-down hex grid. Tasks spawn on left edge, must reach right edge. Drag agent tokens onto hexes: cheap agents (low cost, slow, can't handle hard tasks), expensive agents (high cost, fast, handle anything). Survive 10 waves.
idea.mutation_of: Direct riff on timurtaepov/1078 + vibecoding_tg/3106 signal — agent cost economics is THE current dev meme.
idea.why_novel: Niche/topical theme = strong shareability among the dev-Telegram audience that drives our gallery traffic. Resource-management novel for portfolio.
idea.lose_condition: Token budget hits zero with tasks still in queue

sources.title: Agent cost/economics matters more than raw model quality
sources.snippet: The signal is to reserve expensive models for judgement and use cheaper agents for repetitive automation.

sources.title: Vibe-coding agent cost discussion
sources.snippet: Multi-agent setups making cost economics a daily concern for indie dev teams.

synthesizer_brief: Picked from the daily Telegram digest synthesis. The synthesizer was instructed to mutate trending signals into vanilla-JS canvas mechanics with non-timer pressure.
